frozen_components: LLM used for pairwise self-evaluation and solution generation
architecture.summary: The system trains a verifier model to identify correct solutions among LLM-generated candidates. The core mechanism is REPS (Rationale Enhancement through Pairwise Selection): an LLM iteratively applies pairwise self-evaluation to rank candidate solutions, selecting those with valid rationales. The selected solutions (paired with their rationales) form training data for a separate verifier model, which is then fine-tuned to distinguish sound from flawed reasoning. Information flows from LLM-generation → pairwise self-evaluation scoring → rationale selection → verifier training → verification on held-out candidates.

biblio.title: Rationale-Aware Answer Verification by Pairwise Self-Evaluation

claims.metric: fraction of correct-answer solutions with valid rationales
claims.statement: in StrategyQA, only 19% of LLM-generated solutions with correct answers have valid rationales
training a verifier on valid rationales significantly improves its ability to distinguish valid and flawed rationales

baseline.name: conventional training methods (labeling by final-answer match only)

claims.statement: Verifiers trained on solutions selected by REPS outperform those trained using conventional training methods on three reasoning benchmarks (ARC-Challenge, DROP, and StrategyQA)
REPS selects valid rationales from candidates by iteratively applying pairwise self-evaluation using the same LLM that generates the solutions, without extra human supervision
